main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Discovery Mode Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int discovery_main (void)
Run discovery mode main loop.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Discovery Mode Entry Point

Discovery mode enables zero-configuration video chat where participants can dynamically become hosts based on NAT quality. This implements the "one command to start, one command to join" philosophy.

Discovery Flow

1.
Parse session string (word-word-word format)
2.
Query ACDS for session information
3.
Assess NAT quality (STUN test)
4.
Either: a. Connect to existing host as participant b. Become host if no host exists or better NAT quality
5.
Handle dynamic role changes during session

Implementation Architecture

Discovery mode uses lib/session APIs for media handling:

  • session_host_t: For rendering and broadcasting when elected host
  • session_participant_t: For capture and display when not host
  • discovery_session_t: For ACDS connection and host negotiation

No network protocol code duplication - all media handling via lib/session.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Version

1.0

Definition in file discovery/main.c.

Function Documentation

int discovery_main (void )

Run discovery mode main loop. Discovery mode entry point for unified binary.

Handles session discovery, host negotiation, and media flow.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Definition at line 323 of file discovery/main.c.

323                          {
324   log_debug("discovery_main() starting");
325 
326   // Discovery-specific setup: initialize ACDS connection and session negotiation
327   // Note: Shared setup (keepawake, splash, terminal, capture, display, audio)
328   // is handled by session_client_like_run()
329 
330   // Get session string from options (command-line argument)
331   const char *session_string = GET_OPTION(session_string);
332   bool is_initiator = (session_string == NULL || session_string[0] == '\0');
333 
334   int port_int = GET_OPTION(port);
335 
336   log_debug("Discovery: is_initiator=%d, port=%d", is_initiator, port_int);
337 
338   // Create discovery session configuration
339   discovery_config_t discovery_config = {
340       .acds_address = GET_OPTION(discovery_server),
341       .acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port),
342       .session_string = is_initiator ? NULL : session_string,
343       .local_port = (uint16_t)port_int,
344       .on_state_change = on_discovery_state_change,
345       .on_session_ready = on_session_ready,
346       .on_error = on_discovery_error,
347       .callback_user_data = NULL,
348       .should_exit_callback = discovery_capture_should_exit_adapter,
349       .exit_callback_data = NULL,
350   };
351 
352   // Create discovery session (stored in global for discovery_run() to access)
353   g_discovery = discovery_session_create(&discovery_config);
354   if (!g_discovery) {
355     log_fatal("Failed to create discovery session");
356     return ERROR_MEMORY;
357   }
358 
359   // Start discovery session (connects to ACDS, creates/joins, initiates NAT negotiation)
360   log_debug("Discovery: starting discovery session");
361   asciichat_error_t result = discovery_session_start(g_discovery);
362   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
363     log_fatal("Failed to start discovery session: %d", result);
364     discovery_session_destroy(g_discovery);
365     g_discovery = NULL;
366     return result;
367   }
368 
369   // No network interrupt callback needed - discovery session handles its own shutdown
370   set_interrupt_callback(NULL);
371 
372   /* ========================================================================
373    * Configure and Run Shared Client-Like Session Framework
374    * ========================================================================
375    * session_client_like_run() handles all shared initialization:
376    * - Terminal output management
377    * - Keepawake system
378    * - Splash screen lifecycle
379    * - Media source selection
380    * - FPS probing
381    * - Audio initialization
382    * - Display context creation
383    * - Proper cleanup ordering
384    *
385    * Discovery mode provides:
386    * - discovery_run() callback: NAT negotiation, role determination, media handling
387    * - discovery_keyboard_handler: interactive controls for participant role
388    */
389 
390   session_client_like_config_t config = {
391       .run_fn = discovery_run,
392       .run_user_data = NULL,
393       .tcp_client = NULL,
394       .websocket_client = NULL,
395       .discovery = (void *)g_discovery, // Opaque pointer to discovery session
396       .custom_should_exit = NULL,
397       .exit_user_data = NULL,
398       .keyboard_handler = discovery_keyboard_handler,
399       .max_reconnect_attempts = 0, // Discovery doesn't retry - role is determined once
400       .should_reconnect_callback = NULL,
401       .reconnect_user_data = NULL,
402       .reconnect_delay_ms = 0,
403       .print_newline_on_tty_exit = false, // Server/participant manages cursor
404   };
405 
406   log_debug("Discovery: calling session_client_like_run()");
407   asciichat_error_t session_result = session_client_like_run(&config);
408   log_debug("Discovery: session_client_like_run() returned %d", session_result);
409 
410   // Cleanup discovery session
411   log_debug("Discovery: cleaning up");
412 
413   if (g_discovery) {
414     discovery_session_stop(g_discovery);
415     discovery_session_destroy(g_discovery);
416     g_discovery = NULL;
417   }
418 
419   return (session_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) ? 0 : (int)session_result;
420 }




References discovery_session_create(),
    discovery_session_destroy(), discovery_session_start(),
    discovery_session_stop(), session_client_like_run(), and
    set_interrupt_callback().
